safety_features: Lockout/Tagout electrical disconnect box required
Seal barrier at each conduit going to lift unit (by others)
Grating cover for rolling jack pocket
Maximum anchor bolt torque 175 ft-lbs
No impact wrench for anchor bolt installation
Safety glasses required during installation (ANSI B212.15 drill bits)
notes: Configuration most often used for fleet maintenance with moderate ratio of tire, wheel, or brake services

Allow approximately 60 inches at rear of lift for upward articulation motion
All floor requirements based on concrete slab on grade (supported by soil)

No anchor bolt substitutions permitted without written approval from Mohawk Resources Ltd.
Epoxy grouted threaded rod anchorage may be used under certain circumstances with written approval
Filter/Lubricator/Regulator supplied with lift for locking system only; additional needed for optional accessories
Air consumers should not share auxiliary air supply simultaneously
Oil-water separator required for drain pipe
Provide temporary caps for all conduits and embedded pipes during installation
Control console should be placed far enough away from lift to allow activities around the lift

Lift translates to rear approximately 36 1/8 inches during vertical articulation
Existing concrete floor with f'c >= 4000 psi and >= 6 inches thick may be used without alterations
Floor areas designed for vehicles of same weight as lift unit maximum capacity will be adequate
If floor strength is unknown, core samples should be taken to determine strength
New concrete must be minimum f'c=4000 psi with heavy aggregate, reach full 28-day f'c before anchor bolt installation
For new construction, deepen floor to 9 inches along lift runways and around/rear of service legs
When radiant heating tubes are present, minimum 6 inches clearance required for anchor bolts
Support base plates shall not be installed over construction joints or nearer than 10 inches to free edge
No anchor bolt closer than 10 inches from any free edge or floor joint
All floor requirements based on concrete slab on grade (supported by soil)
Non-grade slabs require engineer/architect review at expense of others

capacity: 14,000 lbs per column (MP-800-014) / 18,000 lbs per column (MP-800-018)

common_specs.touchscreen: 10" touchscreen with diagnostic and browsing capabilities

features: All columns operate together, individually or can be divided into multiple pairs (2, 4, 6 or 8)
Capacities of 56,000 or 72,000 lb for sets of 4
Wireless system operates on broad range of channels to limit interference, with multiple frequencies
Visual indicator lights show real-time charge level status
24 volt operation with built-in 110 volt charging system
69" lifting height in Standard Mode & 32" with two columns in Wheel-Lift mode
Hydraulic operation (no screw drives) for safe, efficient, low maintenance, fast & easy lifting
10" touchscreen control from any column with diagnostic and browsing capabilities
Adjustable lifting forks accommodate rims from 5" to 24.5"
No wheel reducer sleeves needed - adjustable forks
Positive grip ensures carriage forks stay in place when engaged
Low profile fork angle for easy positioning of columns, especially for floatation tires
Mechanical lock ladder - safety latch rack engages locks every 2.5"
Retractable wheel design - more steel on floor for greater stability
Made in U.S.A.

base_plate_anchoring_instructions: 1. Locate posts in desired location of bay, per lift setup dimensions. Refer to lift manual for inside dimensions of columns, etc. (For System IA-10, the inside post dimension is 120")
2. Match drill all base plate holes. Refer to anchor bolt installation specifications attached for drill size. (3/4" diameter original Wej-it style expansion anchor bolts)

3. Install anchors per anchor bolt installation specifications attached. Shim under base plate as needed to ensure that posts are square and plumb (maximum of 1/4") with colored shims provided (horseshoe shaped)
4. All anchors must be a minimum of 6" away from any expansion seam, control joint, or other floor inconsistencies
5. Drilling thru entire slab thickness is preferred when possible. This allows anchors to be hammered flush to concrete

wej_it_anchor_specifications.federal_specs: QQZ-325C Type II Class 3; FFS-325 Group II Type 4 Class 1
installation_steps: Drill hole perpendicular to work surface, do not ream or allow wobble
Drill hole deeper than intended embedment, not closer than 2 anchor diameters to bottom
Clean hole using compressed air and nylon brush
Ensure spear heads are located against wedge pockets
Turn nut until contact with top of spears and bottom of washer, insert into hole
Tap anchor into hole with 2.5 lb hammer until washer rests solidly against fixture
Tighten nut not less than 3 full turns but not more than 5 turns past hand tight
wej_it_anchor_specifications.important: Do NOT use impact wrench for installation

installation_steps: With lift fully lowered, swing all arms to fully wide position providing clear path between lift
Fully extend all arms by pulling sliders to maximum extension on all arms
Place arm covers over all arms, ensure pins go completely thru slider holes and cover sits flat over swing arm tubes
Place troughs over opposing swing arm covers, retain with spring pins in holes provided in swing arm covers
Spread distance between troughs as desired, ensuring retention pin actuates
Always ensure troughs are evenly spread from center of posts

specifications.lifting_mechanism: One hydraulic cylinder resting vertically between jacking beam upper and lower sections
specifications.pump_unit: Air/Hydraulic self-contained modular design with pump submerged into reservoir

specifications.mechanical_locks: Twin mechanical locks, 1.5 inch thick steel each, spring-engage in 3 positions within stroke
specifications.testing: Each unit fully assembled and factory tested prior to shipment

features: Air/Hydraulic operation for wheel free operations on four post track lifts and parallelogram lifts
No cables, chains or mechanical screws used in lifting
Scissoring mechanism provides synchronization for level platform lifting
Rolling axles compress under load for direct frame contact between jack beam frame and lift runways
Modular steel end plates adapt to different end attachments for modularity in design
Spring-loaded heat treated machined rollers with internal needle bearings
Self-contained modular pump design with submerged pump
Manual rocker lever controls for raise, lower, and lock release
Each unit fully assembled and factory tested prior to shipment
Made in the USA
certifications: ANSI/ALI ALCTV-2011 Certified and Listed (when used with compatible Mohawk lifts)
safety_features: Load holding check valve integrated into hydraulic pumping unit
Flow regulating valves mounted in-line with cylinder minimize collapse speed in event of fluid leakage
Twin mechanical locks (1.5 inch thick steel each) spring-engage in 3 positions within stroke
Scissoring mechanism provides synchronization for level platform lifting
Rolling axles compress under load for direct frame contact between jack beam frame and lift runways
Manual lock release lever required to disengage locks for full lowering
notes: Total aggregate capacity of any combination of jacking beams cannot exceed rated capacity of lift
Per ANSI/ALI ALCTV-2011, individual jacking beams are limited to maximum rating of 70% of the capacity of the lift they are installed on
Lifting and lowering speeds are variable, dependent on load applied to jack beam
Mohawk Exclusive Extended Lifetime Cylinder Seal Warranty on hydraulic cylinders

DRILL THE HOLES PERPENDICULAR TO THE WORK SURFACE. *DO ASSURE FULL HOLDING POWER, DO NOT REAM THE HOLE OR ALLOW THE DRILL TO WOBBLE., 2. DRILL THE HOLE NOT DEEPER THAN THE INTENDED EMBEDMENT OF THE ANCHOR, BUT DO NOT DRILL DEEPER THAN TWO ANCHOR DIAMETERS TO THE BOTTOM (OPPOSITE) SURFACE OF THE CONCRETE., 3. CLEAN THE HOLE USING COMPRESSED AIR AND A NYLON BRUSH. A CLEAN HOLE IS NECESSARY FOR PROPER PERFORMANCE., 4. TURN THE NUT ONTO THE ANCHOR BOLT UNTIL CONTACT IS MADE WITH THE TIP OF THE THREADS AND THE BOTTOM OF THE WASHER. INSERT ANCHOR THRU HOLE., 5. TAP ANCHOR INTO HOLE WITH A 3/4 1/2 LBS HAMMER UNTIL WASHER RESTS AGAINST THE CONCRETE., 6. TIGHTEN THE NUT TO 1/2 FT-LBS MAXIMUM TORQUE AND NOT LESS THAN 3 FULL TURNS, BUT NOT MORE THAN 5 TURNS PAST THE HAND TIGHT POSITION. (USE OF AN IMPACT WRENCH FOR INSTALLATION OF ANCHORS IS NOT RECOMMENDED), PACK LEVELING SHIMS IN A STRAIGHT AND COMPACT FASHION SO THE ANCHOR BOLT CAN OBTAIN ITS FULL STRENGTH, USE THIN SHIMS TO FULLY FILL OUT EACH LOCATION., APPROVED ANCHOR BOLTS PROVIDED BY MOHAWK LRT, ANCHOR BOLTS ARE MANUFACTURED BY, WEDGET FASTENING SYSTEMS, 2415 EAST 43rd PLACE, TULSA, OKLAHOMA 74105, 1-800-331-0188, PHONE: 800-331-1344, FAX: 918-622-3559, OR, WEB SITE: www.WEDGIT.COM, ANCHORS SPECIFIED ARE: THE ORIGINAL WEDG-IT EXPANSION ANCHORS, 3/4 DIA, CATALOG NUMBER, LENGTH, 3465, 6, 3482, 8 1/2, 3410, 10, REPRESENTATIVE TIGHTENING SEQUENCE FOR ANCHOR BOLTS TIGHTENING FROM CENTER OF BASE OUTWARD, 2.54, 1.43, APPROVED ANCHOR BOLTS PROVIDED BY MOHAWK LRT, TYPICAL ANCHOR BOLT, 1.14, THE MAXIMUM THICKNESS OF ANY STACK OF SHIMS IS 1, SEE NOTES 9 & 10, 2.20, PLACEMENT OF LEVELING SHIM DETAIL, LEVELING SHIM DETAIL, LEVELING SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE IN A RANGE OF THICKNESSES FROM 1/16, 1/8, & 1/2, *ALWAYS WEAR SAFETY GLASSES. pages_summary.description: General installation notes covering concrete, reinforcement, utilities, and safety requirements
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, FLUSH LIFTS ONLY, NOTE 1: CONCRETE USED FOR THE BASE AND THE SIDE WALLS OF EACH TRENCH AND ANY OTHER NEW CONCRETE WHICH IS USED FOR THIS INSTALLATION MAY HAVE A MINIMUM STRENGTH OF F'c=2,500 psi, A STRENGTH OF F'c=4,000 psi IS RECOMMENDED WHERE POSSIBLE., NOTE 2: CONCRETE USED FOR THE BASE AND SIDEWALLS OF THE TRENCH AREAS SHALL REACH ITS FULL 28 DAY F'c STRENGTH BEFORE THE LIFT AND THE ANCHOR BOLTS ARE INSTALLED., NOTE 3: CONCRETE REINFORCEMENT SIZES AND REINFORCEMENT SPECIFICATION FOR THE BASE OF EACH TRENCH SHALL BE DETERMINED BY AN ENGINEER OR ARCHITECT (AT THE EXPENSE OF THE PURCHASER) AND SHOULD BE DETERMINED CONSIDERING THE LOCAL SOIL CONDITIONS AND THE APPLIED LOADING. AS A MINIMUM, GRADE 60 REINFORCEMENT OF THE SIZE AND SPACING SHOWN IN THE DRAWINGS SHALL BE USED., NOTE 4: CONCRETE REINFORCEMENT SPECIFICATIONS FOR THE FLOOR AREA AROUND THE TRENCHES SHALL BE DETERMINED BY AN ENGINEER OR ARCHITECT (AT THE EXPENSE OF THE PURCHASER) AND SHOULD BE DETERMINED CONSIDERING THE LOCAL SOIL CONDITIONS AND THE APPLIED LOADING. AS A MINIMUM, TWO LAYERS OF GRADE 60, 6x6-10/10 WELDED WIRE FABRIC SHOULD BE USED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT UNIT AND BETWEEN THE TRENCHES., NOTE 5: THE REINFORCING STEEL USED IN THE BASE OF THE TRENCHES SHALL BE INSTALLED SO AS TO NOT INTERFERE WITH THE ANCHOR BOLTS SO TO ATTACH THE LIFT UNIT., NOTE 6: LIFT UNIT INSTALLATION. AT THE TIME OF THE FINAL POSITIONING OF THE LIFT UNIT AND THE LIFT UNIT ON THE FLOOR SURFACE. ALL THE ANCHOR BOLT POSITIONS BOLTS SPECIFIED IN THE DRAWING MUST BE USED. ANCHOR BOLTS OF FULL LENGTH MUST BE USED AT ALL LOCATIONS PROVIDED ON THE BASE OF THE LIFT UNIT., NOTE 7: CARE MUST BE TAKEN TO ENSURE THAT THE SIDE WALLS OF THE TRENCH ARE PARALLEL AND STRAIGHT. APPROXIMATELY 1 1/2 OF TOLERANCE IS PROVIDED ALONG THE SIDES OF THE RUNWAY., NOTE 8: SLOPE THE BOTTOM OF THE TRENCH 1/16 INCH PER FOOT TOWARD THE DRAINAGE CHANNEL. SLOPE THE DRAINAGE CHANNEL 1/16 INCH PER FOOT TOWARD THE CATCH BASIN., NOTE 9: CARE MUST BE TAKEN TO ENSURE THAT THE BASE OF THE TRENCH AREAS ARE AT THE PROPER ELEVATION. A MAXIMUM OF ONE INCH ADJUSTMENT (SHIMMING) IS PERMITTED FOR INSTALLATION LEVELING., NOTE 10: WHERE MORE THAN 3/4 INCH OF SHIM LEVELING IS REQUIRED, FULL SUPPORT PLATE CONTACT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE 1/4 INCH TO 3/4 INCH. THE FLAT WASHER ON A WEDGE TYPE SHIM PLATES SHALL STILL BE IN ACCORD WITH THE 1 INCH OVERALL ADJUSTMENT. THE MAXIMUM ADJUSTMENT CAN STILL BE MADE IN A RANGE COMPRISING THICKNESSES FROM 1/16 TO 1/4 INCH., NOTE 11: NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, EXCEPT THE LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEG CONDUITS (BY MOHAWK), CLOSER THAN 16 INCHES FROM ANY ANCHOR BOLT. ALSO, THE SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED ACCURATELY IN THE LOCATIONS SHOWN IN THE PLAN AND DETAIL VIEWS TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECT ON THE ANCHORAGE., NOTE 12: PROVIDE TWO, 4 INCH SCH 40 PVC PIPE AS A HYDRAULIC-PNEUMATIC SERVICE SUPPLY CONDUIT RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO EACH SERVICE LEG., NOTE 13: PROVIDE 4, 1 INCH SCH 40 STEEL CONDUITS AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO THE SERVICE LEGS. THESE CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED AS SHOWN ON THE SECTION VIEWS AND SHALL BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES., NOTE 14: ONE, 4 INCH SCH 40 PVC DRAIN PIPE SHOULD BE PROVIDED TO CARRY DRAINAGE FROM THE CATCH BASINS TO AN OIL-WATER SEPARATOR. THIS PIPE SHOULD SLOPE A MINIMUM OF 1/16 INCH PER FOOT TOWARD THE DESTINATION., NOTE 15: PROVIDE TEMPORARY CAPS FOR ALL CONDUITS AND EMBEDDED PIPES. IT IS RECOMMENDED TO LEAVE PULL ROPES IN CONDUITS FOR EASE IN LIFT UNIT INSTALLATION., NOTE 16: THE CONTROL CONSOLE MUST BE LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT. IT SHOULD BE PLACED FAR ENOUGH AWAY FROM THE LIFT TO ALLOW FOR ACTIVITIES AROUND THE LIFT. THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS SHOW THE CONSOLE IN A STANDARD POSITION, BUT SEVERAL POSITIONS CAN BE USED. THE CONTROL CONSOLE CAN BE POSITIONED ON EITHER SIDE OF THE LIFT, BUT THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS MAY REQUIRE LOWER GRADES, NOTES, CONDUIT, ETC. IF-AS MENTIONED IN OTHER PLACES., NOTE 17: THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES CLEAN DRY COMPRESSED AIR AT THE PRESSURE AND VOLUME SHOWN ON THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE. A FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR IS SUPPLIED WITH THE LIFT UNIT FOR SHOP LOCKING SYSTEM ONLY. A FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR, AIR DRYER AND SHUTOFF VALVE MUST BE PROVIDED FOR THE LIFT UNIT COMPRESSED AIR SERVICE FOR OPTIONAL JACKING. THE REQUIRED VOLUME OF AIR SHOWN IN THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE RECOGNIZES THAT NOT MORE THAN ONE AUXILIARY AIR CONSUMER WILL BE USED SIMULTANEOUSLY., NOTE 18: PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS A COMPRESSED AIR SUPPLY. THIS CONDUIT IS THE TERMINAL STEEL CONDUIT FROM THE AIR PANEL CONTROL PANEL ASSEMBLY. PVC IS ALLOWED, DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. IF BROUGHT OVERHEAD, PROVIDE FLEX CONDUIT CONNECTING THE TERMINAL STEEL CONDUIT TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE., NOTE 19: THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES A HIGH VOLTAGE POWER SOURCE. A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE PROVIDED FOR THE POWER SOURCE. THE LOCKOUT/TAGOUT DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES. THIS ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT IS TO BE PROVIDED BY OTHERS., NOTE 20: PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE BUILDING POWER SOURCE TO THE CONTROL PANEL. THE CONDUIT IS SHOWN UNDERGROUND, BUT ALTERNATIVELY IT CAN BE BROUGHT OVERHEAD. IF BROUGHT OVERHEAD, PROVIDE FLEX CONDUIT TO CONNECT THE TERMINAL CONDUIT TO THE LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX WITHIN SIGHT AND AS CLOSE TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE AS IS PRACTICAL. THIS ELECTRICAL CONDUIT AND DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO LOCAL ELECTRICAL CODE REQUIREMENTS., NOTE 21: ALL FLOOR REQUIREMENTS ARE BASED ON A CONCRETE SLAB THAT IS ON GRADE (SUPPORTED BY SOIL). ANY OTHER TYPE OF INSTALLATION INVOLVING A SLAB NOT ON GRADE (I.E. SLAB SUPPORTED BY PILINGS, SECOND STORY SLAB, ETC.) MUST BE REVIEWED & ANALYZED FOR SUITABILITY BY THE BUILDING ARCHITECT, AT THE EXPENSE OF OTHERS.

installation_requirements.drainage_slope: 1/16 inch per foot toward drainage channel

notes: Grating is recommended to be used to cover the rolling jack pocket during normal use of the lift unit
This configuration is most often used for fleet maintenance applications that involve a moderate ratio of tire, wheel or brake services
The front of the lift unit is placed to the front of the lift trench
Flush mounted installation with manually covered rolling jack lowering pocket
Concrete used for the base and side walls shall have minimum strength of fc=2,500 psi
All anchor bolts to be wetset anchors with full thread engagement
Care must be taken to ensure trench side walls are parallel and straight
Slope the bottom of trench 1/16 inch per foot toward drainage channel
Maximum one inch adjustment (shimming) is permitted for installation leveling

pages_summary.description: General notes with installation requirements and specifications for surface lifts
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, SURFACE LIFTS ONLY, UNITS = INCH (mm), NOTE 1: IF AN EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A CONCRETE MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NO LESS THAN Fc=4,000 psi (27.6 MPa) AND IS AT LEAST 6 INCHES (152) THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE USED WITHOUT ALTERATIONS, NOTE 12: IN CERTAIN CASES THE FLOOR SLAB MAY HAVE ADEQUATE STRENGTH TO SUPPORT THE LIFT UNIT BUT MAY NOT BE THICK ENOUGH TO PROVIDE THE MINIMUM EMBEDMENT DEPTH FOR THE AT WEJ-IT ANCHOR BOLTS, NOTE 13: EXCEPT AS DESCRIBED IN NOTE 7, NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, EXCEPT THE LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE CLOSER THAN 18 INCHES (406) FROM ANY ANCHOR BOLT, NOTE 14: NO ANCHOR BOLT SHALL BE INSTALLED CLOSER THAN 10 INCHES (254) FROM ANY FREE EDGE OR FLOOR JOINT, NOTE 15: PROVIDE TWO, 4 INCH (102) SCH 40 PVC PIPE AS A HYDRAULIC-PNEUMATIC SERVICE SUPPLY CONDUIT RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO EACH SERVICE LEG, NOTE 16: PROVIDE 4, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 STEEL CONDUITS AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO THE SERVICE LEGS

utilities.electrical: 20 horsepower motor, 30 amperage at 460V 3 ph, 7.69 amp control circuit transformer 1000 VA, 4.8 amp 24 VDC power supply

installation_requirements.drainage: drainage catch basin required, 2.50 inches maximum depth
installation_requirements.conduits: conduits to rear legs of lift platforms, conduits to console

notes: Flush mounted installation with manually covered rolling jack lowering pocket
Most often used for fleet maintenance applications with moderate tire, wheel or undercarriage services
Front of lift unit placed to front of lift pit, cylinders translate to rear as they articulate upward
Approximately 60 inches at rear for HC motion
Grating recommended to cover rolling jack pocket during use of lift unit
Two layers of 6 x 6 10/10 WWF reinforcement required
#4 @ 12 dowels and ties specified
Concrete strength F'c=4,000 psi minimum recommended where possible
All sharp corners & edges unless otherwise specified surface finish to be 125 microinch
Welding return shall conform to AWS specifications to C-max electronics or C-2011 code 3.3 flux core wire only

pages_summary.description: General notes for flush lifts installation requirements and specifications
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, FLUSH LIFTS ONLY, NOTE 1: CONCRETE USED FOR THE BASE AND THE SIDE WALLS OF EACH TRENCH AND ANY OTHER NEW CONCRETE WHICH IS USED FOR THIS INSTALLATION MAY HAVE A MINIMUM STRENGTH OF F'c=4,000 psi, A STRENGTH OF F'c=5,000 psi IS RECOMMENDED WHERE POSSIBLE. NOTE 2: CONCRETE USED FOR THE BASE AND SIDEWALLS OF THE TRENCH AREAS SHALL REACH ITS FULL 28 DAY F'c STRENGTH BEFORE THE LIFT AND ANCHOR BOLTS ARE INSTALLED. NOTE 3: CONCRETE REINFORCEMENT SIZES AND REINFORCEMENT SPECIFICATION FOR THE BASE OF EACH TRENCH SHALL BE DETERMINED BY AN ENGINEER OR ARCHITECT (AT THE EXPENSE OF THE PURCHASER) AND SHOULD BE DETERMINED CONSIDERING THE LOCAL SOIL CONDITIONS AND THE IMPOSED LOADING. AS A MINIMUM, GRADE 60 LAYERS OF GRADE 60, 6X6-10/10 WELDED WIRE FABRIC SHOULD BE USED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT UNIT AND BETWEEN THE TRENCHES.

pages_summary.description: General notes and installation requirements
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, NOTE 1, IF THE EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A DOCUMENTED MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c= 4,000 PSI (28 MPa) AND IS NOT LESS THAN SIX INCHES (152) THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE USED WITHOUT ALTERATIONS. GENERALLY ANY FLOOR AREA WHICH IS DESIGNED FOR VEHICLES OF THE SAME WEIGHT AS THE LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY WILL BE ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT. LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY DATA IS PROVIDED IN THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE, NOTE 2, IF THE CONCRETE FLOOR SYSTEM DOES NOT MEET MINIMUM SPECIFICATIONS OF NOTE 1 ABOVE, THEN A NEW CONCRETE SYSTEM SHALL BE INSTALLED TO SUPPORT THE LIFT, NOTE 3, IF THE STRENGTH OF AN EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM IS UNKNOWN OR NOT DOCUMENTED, ITS STRENGTH SHALL BE DETERMINED. CORE SAMPLES SHOULD BE TAKEN TO DETERMINE THE STRENGTH OF THE FLOOR, NOTE 4, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM SHALL BE AT A MINIMUM F'c=4,000 psi (27.6 MPa), WITH HEAVY AGGREGATE. FOR ANY NEW CONCRETE IT SHALL REACH ITS DESIGN 28 DAY CURE STRENGTH BEFORE THE LIFT AND THE LIFT ANCHORS ARE INSTALLED, NOTE 5, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR FOOTINGS, PADS OR ALTERATIONS SHALL HAVE REINFORCING AS RECOMMENDED BY OTHERS. FOOTINGS AND PADS SHALL CONFORM TO LOCAL BUILDING CODES, CONFORMING OR OTHER, OF A MINIMUM TWO LAYERS OF #4 REBAR 3 INCHES ON CENTER. WELDED WIRE FABRIC SHALL BE USED FOR ANY FLOOR REPAIRS. ALSO, FLOOR REPAIRS SHALL BE DOWELED INTO THE EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM TO PREVENT DIFFERENTIAL SETTLEMENT, NOTE 6, FOR INSTALLATION IN EXISTING STRUCTURES, AREAS OF THE FLOOR WHICH ARE CUT AND REMOVED FOR SERVICE CONDUIT INSTALLATIONS SHALL BE REPAIRED WITH CONCRETE HAVING MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN 4,000 psi (27.6 MPa), WITH AT LEAST 3 INCHES (229) THICK, INTO THE AREAS GROUND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS, NOTE 7, FOR NEW CONSTRUCTION, THE AREAS OF THE FLOOR ALONG THE LIFT RUNWAYS SHOULD BE DEEPENED TO 8 INCHES (203) FOR $8 PER 500 ANCHOR INSTALLATION. ALSO, PROVIDE A MINIMUM OF 1/2 INCH (228) THICKNESS AROUND THE BASE OF LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEGS, NOTE 8, FOR CONSTRUCTION WHERE IN SLAB FLOOR HEATING TUBES ARE USED, THESE TUBES MAY BE PLACED UNDER THE LIFT AREA PROVIDED THE SLAB IS CAST SUFFICIENTLY THICK. A MINIMUM OF 6 INCHES (152) OF CONCRETE SHOULD BE PROVIDED OVER ANCHOR BOLTS AND DRILLING FOR ALIGNMENT. THE INSTALLER SHALL LOCATE THE TUBES PRIOR TO ANCHOR DRILLING TO PREVENT RUPTURE OF THE DEPTH OF THE ANCHORS, NOTE 9, THE SUPPORT PLATES OF THE CONTINUOUS BASE SHALL NOT BE INSTALLED OVER A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OF THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE SUPPORT BASE PLATES SHALL NOT BE PLACED NEARER THAN 10 INCHES (254) TO A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OR FREE EDGE OF THE FLOOR SLAB, NOTE 10, A MAXIMUM OF ONE INCH (25.4) ANCHOR BOLT SHIM THICKNESS IS PERMITTED. INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE IN A RANGE OF THICKNESSES, NOTE 11, WHERE MORE THAN ONE INCH (25.4) OF SHIM LEVELING IS REQUIRED FULL SUPPORT PLATE CONTACT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE AT ADDITIONAL LEVELED COST. FULL CONTACT SHIM PLATES SHALL THEN BE ACCOMPLISHED BY EPOXY USING INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS, NOTE 12, WEJ-IT FASTENING SYSTEMS, AT WEDGE ANCHORS ARE PROVIDED WITH THE LIFT FOR ANCHORING THE LIFT UNIT TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE NUMBER AND THE SIZE OF ANCHOR BOLTS SPECIFIED IN THE DRAWING MUST BE USED TO ATTACH THE LIFT UNIT. ANCHOR BOLTS OF FULL LENGTH MUST BE USED IN ALL LOCATIONS PROVIDED ON THE BASE OF THE LIFT UNIT, SURFACE LIFTS ONLY, UNITS = INCH (mm), NOTE 12, IN CERTAIN CASES THE FLOOR SLAB MAY HAVE ADEQUATE STRENGTH TO SUPPORT THE LIFT UNIT BUT MAY NOT BE THICK ENOUGH TO PROVIDE THE MINIMUM EMBEDMENT DEPTH FOR THE AT TYPICAL ANCHOR BOLTS. IN CASES SUCH AS THESE SHORTER ANCHOR BOLTS MAY BE USED CONTACT MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD. FOR WRITTEN APPROVAL OF EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS FOR LIFT APPLICATIONS AND APPROPRIATE INSTALLATION FOR LIFT UNIT, NOTE 13, EXCEPT AS DESCRIBED IN NOTE 7, NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, EXCEPT THE LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE CLOSER THAN 16 INCHES (406) FROM ANY ANCHOR BOLT. ALSO, ALL SERVICE LEG CONDUITS MUST BE INSTALLED ACCURATELY IN THE LOCATIONS SHOWN IN THE PLAN AND DETAIL VIEWS TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECT ON THE ANCHORAGE, NOTE 14, NO ANCHOR BOLT SHALL BE INSTALLED CLOSER THAN 10 INCHES (254) FROM ANY FREE EDGE OR FLOOR JOINT, NOTE 15, PROVIDE 4 INCH (102) SCH 40 PVC PIPE AS A HYDRAULIC—PNEUMATIC SERVICE SUPPLY CONDUIT RUNNING FROM POWER UNIT TO EACH SERVICE LEG, NOTE 16, PROVIDE 4, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 STEEL CONDUITS AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE CONTROL TO EACH SERVICE LEG. THESE CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED AS SHOWN ON THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS AND MUST BE IN THE PRECISE LOCATIONS SPECIFIED TO ACCOMMODATE, NOTE 17, PROVIDE TEMPORARY CAPS FOR ALL CONDUITS AND EMBEDDED PIPES. IT IS RECOMMENDED TO LEAVE PULL ROPES IN CONDUITS FOR EASE OF LIFT INSTALLATION, NOTE 18, THE CONTROL CONSOLE MUST BE LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT. IT SHOULD BE PLACED FAR ENOUGH FROM THE LIFT TO ALLOW FOR VEHICLE LOADING THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS SHOW THE CONTROL CONSOLE IN A STANDARD POSITION. THE CONTROL CONSOLE MAY BE LOCATED ON EITHER SIDE AND ANYWHERE ALONG THE LENGTH OF THE LIFT, BUT ANY DEVIATIONS FROM THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS MAY REQUIRE LONGER CABLES, HOSES, CONDUIT, ETC. ADDITIONAL EXPENSE TO THE PURCHASER, NOTE 19, THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES A HIGH VOLTAGE POWER SOURCE. A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE PROVIDED FOR POWER SOURCE. THE LOCKOUT/TAGOUT DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES. THIS ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT IS TO BE PROVIDED BY OTHERS, NOTE 20, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE MAIN ELECTRICAL PANEL TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE. THIS CONDUIT MUST BE RUN UNDERGROUND ALTERNATELY IT MAY BE BROUGHT TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. PROVIDE A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX AND WIRE AS CLOSE TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE AS IS PRACTICAL. THIS ELECTRICAL SUPPLY CONDUIT AND DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO LOCAL ELECTRICAL CODE REQUIREMENTS, NOTE 21, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS A COMPRESSED AIR SUPPLY. THIS CONDUIT IS SHOWN UNDERGROUND, ALTERNATIVELY IT MAY BE BROUGHT TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD, DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. IF BROUGHT OVERHEAD, PROVIDE FLEX CONDUIT CONNECTING THE TERMINAL END OF THE CONDUIT TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE, NOTE 22, THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES CLEAN DRY COMPRESSED AIR AT THE PRESSURE AND VOLUME SHOWN ON THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE. A FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR IS SUPPLIED WITH THE LIFT UNIT FOR THE LIFT. UNIT OWNER IS RESPONSIBLE FOR PROVIDING MAIN SUPPLY AIR SUITABLE FOR THE UNIT SUPPLIED FOR THE LIFT UNIT TO OPERATE THE OPTIONAL ACCESSORIES. THE REQUIRED VOLUME OF AIR SHOWN IN THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE RECOMMENDS THAT MORE THAN ONE AUXILIARY AIR CONSUMER WILL BE USED SIMULTANEOUSLY, NOTE 23, ALL FLOOR REQUIREMENTS ARE BASED ON A CONCRETE SLAB THAT IS ON GRADE (SUPPORTED BY SOIL). ANY OTHER TYPE OF INSTALLATION INVOLVING A SLAB NOT ON GRADE (I.E.—SLAB SUPPORTED BY PYLONS, SECOND STORY SLAB, ETC.) MUST BE REVIEWED & ANALYZED FOR SUITABILITY BY THE BUILDING ENGINEER OR ARCHITECT OF RECORD BY OTHERS, MOHAWK RESOURCES LTD., SURFACE INSTALLATION GENERAL NOTES

pages_summary.description: General installation notes and surface lift requirements
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, NOTE 1: IF THE EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A DOCUMENTED MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c= 4,000 psi AND IS AT LEAST 6 INCHES THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE CONSIDERED ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT PROVIDED THE TOTAL WEIGHT OF THE LIFT UNIT AS THE LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY WILL BE ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT, LIFT UNIT CONTACT PRESSURES WILL BE SPREAD OVER THE BASE PLATES., SURFACE LIFTS ONLY, NOTE 12: IN CERTAIN CASES THE FLOOR SLAB MAY HAVE ADEQUATE STRENGTH TO SUPPORT THE LIFT UNIT BUT MAY NOT BE THICK ENOUGH TO PROVIDE THE MINIMUM EMBEDMENT DEPTH FOR THE AT FULL ANCHOR BOLTS., NOTE 13: EXCEPT AS DESCRIBED IN NOTE 7, NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, EXCEPT THE LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE CLOSER THAN 16 INCHES FROM ANY ANCHOR BOLT., NOTE 14: NO ANCHOR BOLT SHALL BE INSTALLED CLOSER THAN 10 INCHES FROM ANY FREE EDGE OR FLOOR JOINT., NOTE 15: PROVIDE TWO, 4 INCH SCH 40 PVC PIPE AS A HYDRAULIC-PNEUMATIC SERVICE SUPPLY CONDUIT RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO EACH SERVICE LEG., NOTE 16: PROVIDE 4, 1 INCH SCH 40 STEEL CONDUITS AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO THE SERVICE LEGS., NOTE 17: PROVIDE TEMPORARY CAPS FOR ALL CONDUITS AND EMBEDDED PIPES., NOTE 18: THE CONTROL CONSOLE MUST BE LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT., NOTE 19: THE BUILDING ELECTRICAL SUPPLY THAT SUPPLIES A HIGH VOLTAGE POWER SOURCE., NOTE 20: PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE BUILDING POWER SOURCE TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE., NOTE 21: PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS A COMPRESSED AIR SUPPLY., NOTE 22: THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES CLEAN DRY COMPRESSED AIR AT THE PRESSURE AND VOLUME SHOWN ON THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE., NOTE 23: ALL FLOOR REQUIREMENTS ARE BASED ON A CONCRETE SLAB THAT IS ON GRADE (SUPPORTED BY SOIL).

notes: Surface mounted continuous base installation
This configuration is most often used for fleet maintenance, repair and service applications
The lift unit translates to the rear approximately 36 1/8 inches during the vertical articulation
Use smooth electrical 90's in conduits, not plumbing 90's
Remove all sharp corners & edges unless otherwise specified, surface finish to be 125 RMS
Welding medium shall conform to AWS specifications to E-70XX electrodes or E-7011 core 5.3 flux core wire only
All floor strength requirements are based on a concrete slab that is on grade (supported by soil)
The control console must be located in the vicinity of the lift

installation_requirements.concrete_strength: F'c=4,000 psi minimum, F'c=6,000 psi recommended where possible
installation_requirements.pit_reinforcement: Two layers of Grade 60 rebar should be used in the vicinity of the lift unit and between the trenches

electrical_requirements.voltage: 208/230 V 3 PH or 460 V 3 PH or 575 V 3 PH

installation_requirements.drainage: 2.50 inches (64 mm) maximum depth at catch basin, slope 1/16 inch per foot toward drainage channel

notes: Flush mounted installation with manually covered rolling jack lowering pocket
This configuration is most often used for fleet maintenance applications that involve a moderate ratio of tire, wheel or brake services
The front of the lift unit is placed to the front of the lift trench
Grating is recommended to be used to cover the rolling jack pocket during use of the lift unit
All floor requirements are based on a concrete slab that is on grade (supported by soil)
Concrete used for this installation may have a minimum strength of F'c=4,000 psi
Materials shown on this list shall be used without substitution unless specifically approved in writing by Mohawk Resources, Ltd.

notes: Surface installation general notes - this configuration is most often used for fleet maintenance, repair and service applications
Runway and plates not shown, for clarity
Use smooth electrical 90's in conduits, not plumbing 90's
Control console must be located in the vicinity of the lift
Floor slab may have adequate strength to support lift unit but may not be thick enough to provide minimum depth for the automotive lift anchor bolts
Contact Mohawk Resources Ltd. for written approval of epoxy grouted anchors
No anchor bolt shall be installed closer than 10 inches from any free edge or floor joint
Provide 4 inch PVC pipe as hydraulic-pneumatic service supply conduit
Provide 4 1 inch steel conduits as electrical service supply
All floor requirements are based on concrete slab that is on grade supported by soil

pages_summary.description: General notes and surface lift installation requirements
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, NOTE 1, IF THE EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A DOCUMENTED MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c= 4,000 psi (27.6 MPa) AND IS AT LEAST 6 INCHES (152) THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE USED WITHOUT ALTERATIONS. GENERALLY ANY FLOOR AREA WHICH IS DESIGNED FOR VEHICLES OF THE SAME WEIGHT CLASS WILL BE ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THIS TYPE OF AUTOMOTIVE LIFT UNIT. CONTACT MOHAWK RESOURCES IF FLOOR IS UNKNOWN., NOTE 2, IF THE CONCRETE FLOOR SYSTEM DOES NOT MEET MINIMUM SPECIFICATIONS OF NOTE 1 ABOVE, THEN A NEW CONCRETE FLOOR SHALL BE INSTALLED TO SUPPORT THE LIFT., NOTE 3, IF THE STRENGTH OF AN EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM IS UNKNOWN OR NOT DOCUMENTED, ITS STRENGTH SHALL BE DETERMINED BY QUALIFIED CORE SAMPLES TO BE TAKEN TO DETERMINE THE STRENGTH OF THE FLOOR., NOTE 4, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM SHALL BE AT A MINIMUM F'c=4,000 psi (27.6 MPa), WITH HEAVY AGGREGATE. FOR ANY NEW CONCRETE IT SHALL REACH ITS FULL 28 DAY F'c STRENGTH BEFORE THE LIFT AND ITS ANCHOR BOLTS ARE INSTALLED., NOTE 5, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR FLOOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS SHALL HAVE REINFORCING AS REQUIRED FOR THE SOIL CONDITIONS AND SERVICE LOAD LEVEL. THE REINFORCING SHALL BE DESIGNED BY A QUALIFIED STRUCTURAL ENGINEER. WELDED WIRE MESH REINFORCING FABRIC SHALL BE USED FOR ANY FLOOR REPAIRS. ALSO, FLOOR REPAIRS SHALL BE DOWELED INTO THE EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM TO PREVENT DIFFERENTIAL SETTLEMENT., NOTE 6, FOR INSTALLATION IN EXISTING STRUCTURES, AREAS OF THE FLOOR WHICH ARE CUT AND REMOVED FOR SERVICE CONDUIT INSTALLATIONS SHALL BE REPAIRED WITH CONCRETE HAVING MINIMUM STRENGTH NOT LESS THAT F'c=4,000 psi (27.6 MPa), AND SHALL BE AT LEAST 9 INCHES (229) WIDE TO THE AREAS AROUND AND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS., NOTE 7, FOR NEW CONSTRUCTION, THE AREAS OF THE FLOOR ALONG THE LIFT RUNWAYS SHOULD BE DEEPENED TO A MINIMUM OF 9 INCHES (229) TO EASE FLAT ANCHOR INSTALLATION. ALSO, PROVIDE A MINIMUM OF 3 FOOT WIDE AREAS AROUND AND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS., NOTE 8, FOR NEW CONSTRUCTION, WHERE IN FLOOR SERVICE HEATING TUBES ARE USED, THESE TUBES MAY BE PLACED UNDER THE LIFT AREA PROVIDED THE SLAB IS CAST SUFFICIENTLY THICK. A MINIMUM OF 6 INCHES (152) CLEARANCE SHOULD BE PROVIDED TO THE TOP OF ANCHOR BOLTS AND DRILLING EQUIPMENT. ALSO, REMEMBER THAT CONDUIT TUBES ARE NEEDED SOUR STUB CODE OFTEN NOT TO EXCEED WHILE THE DEPTH OF THE ANCHORS., NOTE 9, THE SUPPORT PLATES OF THE CONTINUOUS BASE SHALL NOT BE INSTALLED OVER A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OF THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE SUPPORT BASE SHALL NOT BE PLACED NEARER THAN 10 INCHES (254) TO A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OR FREE EDGE OF THE FLOOR SLAB., NOTE 10, A MAXIMUM OF ONE INCH (25.4) ANCHOR BOLT SHIM THICKNESS IS PERMITTED. INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE IN A RANGE OF THICKNESSES., NOTE 11, WHERE MORE THAN ONE INCH (25.4) OF SHIM LEVELING IS REQUIRED FULL SUPPORT PLATE CONTACT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE AT ADDITIONAL COST. FULL CONTACT SHIM PLATES SHALL THEN BE ACCURATELY LEVELED USING INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS., NOTE 11, WEJ-IT FASTENING SYSTEMS. AT WEDGE ANCHORS ARE PROVIDED WITH THE LIFT FOR ANCHORING THE LIFT UNIT TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE NUMBER AND THE SIZE OF ANCHOR BOLTS DESCRIBED IN THE DRAWING MUST BE USED TO ATTACH THE LIFT UNIT. ANCHOR BOLTS OF FULL LENGTH MUST BE USED IN ALL LOCATIONS PROVIDED FOR AT THE BASE OF THE LIFT UNIT., SURFACE LIFTS ONLY, UNITS = INCH (mm), NOTE 12, IN CERTAIN CASES THE FLOOR SLAB MAY HAVE ADEQUATE STRENGTH TO SUPPORT THE LIFT UNIT BUT MAY NOT BE THICK ENOUGH TO PROVIDE THE MINIMUM DEPTH FOR THE AUTOMOTIVE LIFT ANCHOR BOLTS. FOR WRITTEN APPROVAL OF EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS CONTACT MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD. FOR WRITTEN APPROVAL OF EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS AND PROCEDURES AND SPECIFICATIONS REQUIRED FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT., NOTE 13, EXCEPT AS DESCRIBED IN NOTE 7, NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, EXCEPT THE LIFT UNIT SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE CLOSER THAN 16 INCHES (406) FROM ANY ANCHOR BOLT. ALSO, THE SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED ACCURATELY IN THE LOCATIONS SHOWN IN THE PLAN AND DETAIL VIEWS TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECT ON THE ANCHORAGE., NOTE 14, NO ANCHOR BOLT SHALL BE INSTALLED CLOSER THAN 10 INCHES (254) FROM ANY FREE EDGE OR FLOOR JOINT., NOTE 15, PROVIDE TWO, 4 INCH (102) SCH 40 PVC PIPE AS A HYDRAULIC-PNEUMATIC SERVICE SUPPLY CONDUIT RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO EACH SERVICE LEG., NOTE 16, PROVIDE 4, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 STEEL CONDUITS AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO THE SERVICE LEGS. THESE CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED AS SHOWN ON THE DRAWINGS. IF BROUGHT UNDERGROUND THE BURIED SECTIONS SHALL CONFORM TO THE NEC., NOTE 17, PROVIDE TEMPORARY CAPS FOR ALL CONDUITS AND EMBEDDED PIPES. IT IS RECOMMENDED TO LEAVE PULL ROPES IN CONDUITS FOR EASE OF LIFT INSTALLATION., NOTE 18, THE CONTROL CONSOLE MUST BE LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT. IT SHOULD BE PLACED FAR ENOUGH AWAY FROM THE LIFT TO ALLOW FOR ACTIVITIES AROUND THE LIFT. THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS SHOW THE CONSOLE IN A STANDARD POSITION. THE CONTROL CONSOLE MAY BE LOCATED ON EITHER SIDE AND ANYWHERE ALONG THE LENGTH OF THE LIFT, BUT ANY DEVIATIONS FROM THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS MAY REQUIRE LONGER CABLES, HOSES, CONDUIT, ETC. ADDITIONAL EXPENSE TO THE PURCHASER., NOTE 19, THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES A HIGH VOLTAGE POWER SOURCE. A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT SWITCH MUST BE PROVIDED FOR THE POWER SOURCE. THE LOCKOUT/TAGOUT DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES. THIS ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT IS TO BE PROVIDED BY OTHERS., NOTE 20, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX TO THE CONTROL PANEL. THIS CONDUIT IS SHOWN UNDERGROUND. ALTERNATIVELY IT MAY BE BROUGHT TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. PROVIDE A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX WITH RIGHT HAND SIDE AS CLOSE TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE AS IS PRACTICAL. THIS ELECTRICAL SUPPLY CONDUIT AND DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO LOCAL ELECTRICAL CODE REQUIREMENTS., NOTE 21, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH (25.4) SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS A COMPRESSED AIR SUPPLY. THIS CONDUIT IS SHOWN UNDERGROUND. ALTERNATIVELY IT MAY BE BROUGHT TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. IF BROUGHT OVERHEAD, PROVIDE FLEX CONDUIT CONNECTING THE VERTICAL END OF THE CONDUIT TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE., NOTE 22, THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES CLEAN DRY COMPRESSED AIR AT THE PRESSURE AND VOLUME SHOWN ON THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE. A FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR IS SUPPLIED WITH THE LIFT UNIT FOR THE LIFT UNIT TO OPERATE. THE FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR REQUIRES THAT NO MORE THAN ONE AUXILIARY AIR CONSUMER WILL BE USED SIMULTANEOUSLY. PROVIDED FOR THE LIFT UNIT TO OPERATE THE OPTIONAL ACCESSORIES. THE REQUIRED VOLUME OF AIR SHOWN IN THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE RECOGNIZES THAT NO MORE THAN ONE AUXILIARY AIR CONSUMER WILL BE USED SIMULTANEOUSLY., NOTE 23, ALL FLOOR REQUIREMENTS ARE BASED ON A CONCRETE SLAB THAT IS ON GRADE (SUPPORTED BY SOIL). ANY OTHER TYPE OF INSTALLATION INVOLVING A SLAB NOT ON GRADE (I.E.—SLAB SUPPORTED BY PYLONS, SECOND STORY SLAB, ETC.) MUST BE REVIEWED & ANALYZED FOR SUITABILITY BY THE BUILDING ARCHITECT. —SEE ADVICE OF OTHERS—, MOHAWK RESOURCES LTD.

pages_summary.description: General installation notes and requirements
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, NOTE 1, IF THE EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A DOCUMENTED MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c= 4,000 psi AND IS AT LEAST 6 INCHES THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE USED WITHOUT ALTERATION. GENERALLY ANY FLOOR AREA WHICH IS DESIGNED FOR VEHICLES EQUAL TO OR GREATER THAN THE LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY WILL BE ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT, LIFT UNIT CONTACT PRESSURES ON THE FLOOR ARE LESS THAN THE WHEEL CONTACT PRESSURES, NOTE 2, IF THE CONCRETE FLOOR SYSTEM DOES NOT MEET MINIMUM SPECIFICATIONS OF NOTE 1 ABOVE, THEN A NEW CONCRETE FLOOR SHALL BE INSTALLED TO SUPPORT THE LIFT, NOTE 3, IF THE STRENGTH OF AN EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM IS UNKNOWN OR NOT DOCUMENTED, ITS STRENGTH SHOULD BE DETERMINED. CORE SAMPLES SHOULD BE TAKEN TO DETERMINE THE STRENGTH OF THE FLOOR, NOTE 4, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM SHALL BE AT A MINIMUM F'c=4,000 psi, WITH HEAVY AGGREGATE, FOR ANY NEW CONCRETE IT SHALL REACH ITS FULL 28 DAY COMPRESSIVE STRENGTH BEFORE THE LIFT AND THE ANCHOR BOLTS ARE INSTALLED, NOTE 5, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR FLOOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS SHALL HAVE REINFORCING AS REQUIRED FOR THE SOIL CONDITIONS AND VEHICLE LOAD LEVEL. THE REINFORCING SHALL BE DETERMINED BY OTHERS. AT A MINIMUM #4 BARS AT 12" O.C. EACH WAY, BUT 6 10/10 WELDED WIRE FABRIC SHALL BE USED FOR ANY FLOOR REPAIRS. ALSO, FLOOR REPAIRS SHALL BE DOWELED INTO THE EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM TO PREVENT DIFFERENTIAL SETTLEMENT, NOTE 6, FOR INSTALLATION IN EXISTING STRUCTURES, AREAS OF THE FLOOR WHICH ARE CUT AND REMOVED FOR SERVICE LEG INSTALLATIONS SHALL BE REPAIRED WITH CONCRETE HAVING MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c=4,000 psi AND AT LEAST 6" THICK IN THE AREAS SURROUNDING AND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS, NOTE 7, TO ALLOW FOR CONSTRUCTION, THE AREAS OF EACH FLOOR ALONG THE LIFT RUNWAYS SHOULD BE DEEPENED TO 9 INCHES FOR EASE OF ANCHOR INSTALLATION. ALSO, THE AREA OF THE SERVICE LEGS SHOULD PROVIDE A MINIMUM 9 INCH THICKNESS AROUND AND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS, FOR NEW CONSTRUCTION WHERE IN FLOOR RADIANT HEATING TUBES ARE USED, THESE TUBES MAY BE PLACED UNDER THE LIFT AREA PROVIDED THE THE SLAB IS CAST SUFFICIENTLY THICK. A MINIMUM OF 6 INCHES CLEARANCE SHOULD BE PROVIDED FOR ANCHOR BOLTS AND DRILLING ALLOTMENT. THE INSTALLER MUST BE NOTIFIED THAT RADIANT TUBES ARE TO BE USED AND MUST TAKE CARE IS TAKEN NOT TO OVER DRILL AND COMPROMISE THE DEPTH OF THE ANCHORS, NOTE 8, THE SUPPORT PLATES OF THE CONTINUOUS BASE SHALL NOT BE INSTALLED OVER A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OF THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE SUPPORT PLATES SHALL NOT BE PLACED NEARER THAN 10 INCHES FROM A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OR FREE EDGE OF THE FLOOR SLAB, NOTE 9, A MAXIMUM OF ONE INCH ANCHOR BOLT SHIM THICKNESS IS PERMITTED. INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE IN A RANGE OF THICKNESSES, NOTE 10, WHEN MORE THAN ONE INCH OF SHIM LEVELING IS REQUIRED FULL SUPPORT PLATE CONTACT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE AT ADDITIONAL COST. THE FULL CONTACT SHIM PLATES SHALL THEN BE ACCURATELY LEVELED USING INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS, NOTE 11, WEJ-IT FASTENING SYSTEMS, AT WEDGE ANCHORS ARE PROVIDED WITH THE LIFT FOR ANCHORING THE LIFT UNIT TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE NUMBER AND THE SIZE OF ANCHOR BOLTS SPECIFIED IN THE DRAWING MUST BE USED TO ATTACH THE LIFT UNIT. ANCHOR BOLTS OF FULL LENGTH MUST BE USED AT ALL LOCATIONS PROVIDED ON THE BASE OF THE LIFT UNIT, NOTE 12, IN CERTAIN CASES THE FLOOR SLAB MAY HAVE ADEQUATE STRENGTH TO SUPPORT THE LIFT UNIT BUT MAY NOT BE THICK ENOUGH TO PROVIDE THE MINIMUM EMBEDMENT DEPTH FOR THE AT WEJ-IT ANCHOR BOLTS. IN THIS SITUATION EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS MAY BE USED, CONTACT MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD. FOR WRITTEN APPROVAL OF EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS AND PROCEDURES AND APPROVED METHODS FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT, NOTE 13, EXCEPT AS DESCRIBED IN NOTE 2, NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, SHALL BE PERMITTED IN THE FLOOR UNDER THE LIFT UNIT INSTALLATION WHICH COULD BE DAMAGED BY ANCHOR BOLT HOLE DRILLING. pages_summary.extracted_text: MOHAWK MODEL RJ-50-AH SERIES AIR/HYDRAULIC ROLLING JACK BEAM Air / Hydraulic Rolling Jack Beam for wheel free operations on four post track lifts and parallelogram lifts. THIS LIFT OPTION IS CERTIFIED AND LISTED TO ANSI/ALI ALCTV-2011 when used in conjunction with Mohawk Lift models: TR-30, TR-33, TR-35, TR-50, TR-75 or Mohawk Parallelogram Lifts. CAPACITY* Model RJ-50-AH-50000: Rated 50,000 Lb on 75k Lb Lifts (4 Post) Model RJ-50-AH-37500-P: Rated 37,500 Lb on 75-100k Lb Lifts (Parallel) Model RJ-50-AH-35000: Rated 35,000 Lb on 50k Lb Lifts (4 Post) Model RJ-50-AH-35000-P: Rated 35,000 Lb on 50k Lb Lifts (Parallel) Model RJ-50-AH-31500-P: Rated 31,500 Lb on 45k Lb Lifts (Parallel) Model RJ-50-AH-28000-P: Rated 28,000 Lb on 40k Lb Lifts (Parallel) Model RJ-50-AH-25000-P: Rated 25,000 Lb on 36-38k Lb Lifts (Parallel) Model RJ-50-AH-24500: Rated 24,500 Lb on 35k Lb Lifts (4-Post) Model RJ-50-AH-23000: Rated 23,000 Lb on 33k Lb Lifts (4-Post) Model RJ-50-AH-20000: Rated 20,000 Lb on 30k Lb Lifts (4-Post) BEAM LENGTH 54-1/2" (TR-33/3/5/50/75), 51" (Parallelogram) BEAM DEPTH 28-1/2" BEAM HEIGHT 15-1/2" BEAM HEIGHT ABOVE TRACK (RETRACTED) 2 1/2" (TR-33/35/50/75), 3 7/8" (Parallelogram) LIFTING STROKE 8" LIFTING SPEED 150 Seconds ** LOWERING SPEED 100 Seconds ** AIR SUPPLY RECOMMENDED 100-120 PSI, 10 CFM MIN SHIPPING WEIGHT 850 Lbs. *The total aggregate capacity of any combination of jacking beams cannot exceed rated capacity of lift. Note that per ANSI/ALI ALCTV-2011, individual jacking beams are limited to a maximum rating of 70% of the capacity of the lift they are installed on. **variable speed, dependent on load applied to jack beam. STANDARD EQUIPMENT Flat Lifting Pads (2) Axle Lifting Pads (2) 5" Stackable Height Adapters (2) 7 1/2" Stackable Height Adapters (2) 10" Stackable Height Adapters (2) LIFTING MECHANISM One hydraulic cylinder resting vertically between jacking beam upper and lower sections. Mohawk's Exclusive Extended Lifetime Cylinder Warranty on the hydraulic cylinders. There are no cables, chains or mechanical screws used in lifting.

pages_summary.description: Detailed system specifications including safety systems, controls, construction details, and warranty information
pages_summary.extracted_text: HYDRAULIC SAFETY SYSTEM Load holding check valve integrated into hydraulic pumping unit. Flow regulating valves mounted in-line with cylinder minimize collapse speed in the event of fluid leakage. MECHANICAL SAFETY SYSTEM Twin Mechanical locks (1 ½ inch thick steel, each) spring-engage in (3) positions within the stroke of the beam. Scissoring mechanism provides synchronization for level platform lifting. Rolling axles compress under load to provide direct frame contact between the jack beam frame and the lift runways. SYSTEM CONTROLS Manual rocker lever to actuate air/hydraulic pump unit to raise beam deck. Manual rocker lever to actuate deck lowering onto mechanical locks, and a manual lock release lever to disengage locks for full lowering. PUMP UNIT Air/Hydraulic self-contained modular design with pump submerged into reservoir. Two gallon capacity steel reservoir. Recommended operating temperature range 50 to 130 degrees F. Hydraulic fluid medium: Dexron III. JACK FRAME CONSTRUCTION Two (2) 1/2" wall high strength steel formed sections, one upper and one lower, reinforced with supporting steel braces. Modular steel end plates adapt to different end attachments for modularity in design. AXLES / BEARINGS Four (4) steel axles are provided with (4) matching spring-loaded heat treated machined rollers with internal needle bearings. These rollers ride along the integral steel railing provided on the inner runways. MANUFACTURING SPECS Meets or exceeds ANSI/ALI ALCTV-2011. Made with pride in the USA. TESTING AND ASSEMBLY Each and every unit is fully assembled and factory tested prior to shipment. WARRANTY Mohawk's Exclusive Extended Lifetime Cylinder Seal Warranty on the hydraulic cylinders. SALES, INSTALLATION, TRAINING, & SERVICE Only by experienced, factory authorized professionals.
